# obsidian-mcp-rs

> Rust-based MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for Obsidian vaults. Lets AI assistants (Claude, Cursor, etc.) read, write, search, and manage notes inside Obsidian vaults over stdio transport.

## What it does

- Exposes 11 MCP tools for full note lifecycle management: create, read, edit, delete, move, search, tag
- Supports multiple vaults simultaneously via CLI arguments
- Ships as a single static binary — no runtime required
- Distributed as an npm package (`obsidian-mcp-rs`) with platform-specific binaries via optional dependencies

## Tools

- `read-note` — read note content by filename and optional folder
- `create-note` — create a new markdown note with content
- `edit-note` — append, prepend, replace, or find-and-replace in a note
- `delete-note` — delete a note
- `move-note` — move or rename a note within a vault
- `create-directory` — create a directory inside a vault
- `search-vault` — search notes by content, filename, or tag (`tag:` prefix)
- `list-available-vaults` — list all configured vaults
- `add-tags` — add tags to frontmatter and/or content
- `remove-tags` — remove tags from a note
- `rename-tag` — rename a tag across all notes in a vault

## Usage

```bash
npx obsidian-mcp-rs /path/to/vault
```

MCP config (Claude Desktop / Cursor / Claude Code):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "obsidian": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "obsidian-mcp-rs", "/path/to/vault"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Tech stack

- Language: Rust (stable)
- MCP library: rmcp 2.2 (stdio transport)
- CLI: clap
- Distributed via npm optional dependencies pattern

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/obsidian-mcp-rs
- GitHub: https://github.com/MrRefactoring/obsidian-mcp-rs
- Issues: https://github.com/MrRefactoring/obsidian-mcp-rs/issues
